w20: Accelerator-optimized instance with dual NVIDIA A100 GPUs and 80GB VRAM, designed for intensive machine learning and large language model inference tasks.
c150: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-2g: Accelerator-optimized instance featuring dual NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 24 vCPUs, and 170GB RAM. Ideal for advanced AI/ML workloads.
c200: Explore Google Cloud Platform's a2-highgpu-2g, an accelerator-optimized instance. It features 2 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 24 x86_64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 170GB RAM, ideal for intensive AI/ML tasks.
w150: Google Cloud Platform's a2-highgpu-2g is an accelerator-optimized instance, built on an x86_64 Intel Xeon CPU architecture. It provides 24 virtual CPUs across 12 physical cores, clocked at 2.2 GHz, and is provisioned with 170 GB of system memory. The server is equipped with two NVIDIA A100 GPUs, collectively offering 80 GB of VRAM. No local storage is bundled with this configuration. CPU benchmarks indicate weak single-core performance (bottom 25%) and average multi-core performance (middle 50%), with memory bandwidth also registering as average. However, the instance excels in GPU-intensive tasks, particularly large language model inference, achieving top-tier performance across various model sizes. For instance, it processes small model prompts at 6477.28 tokens/sec and generates text for large models at 22.39 tokens/sec. This makes it highly suitable for demanding machine learning, deep learning training, and large-scale AI inference applications.
